w20: Dedicated GPU acceleration with 80GB VRAM, ideal for demanding AI and machine learning workloads requiring high-speed inference and data processing.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g: Accelerator-optimized server with 1 NVIDIA A100 80GB GPU, 12 vCPUs, and 170GB RAM for advanced AI/ML.
c200: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-1g offers dedicated GPU acceleration with an NVIDIA A100 80GB GPU, 12 vCPUs, and 170GB RAM. Optimized for high-performance AI, machine learning, and data science workloads.
w150: Google Cloud Platform's a2-ultragpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server, built on an x86_64 architecture. It features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, operating at 2.2 GHz across 6 physical cores with 2 threads per core, and is provisioned with 170 GB of memory. The server integrates a single NVIDIA A100 GPU, offering 80 GB of VRAM, and includes 375 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth, with single-core CPU and compression tasks showing weaker results. Despite this, the system demonstrates top-tier LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes, including large 70B models. This configuration is specifically engineered for demanding data science jobs, intensive machine learning training, and high-throughput LLM inference applications where GPU acceleration is paramount.
